name: Bernard Addison
abstract: Bernard Sylvester Addison (April 15, 1905 – December 18, 1990) was an American jazz guitarist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra with Benny Carter and The Swing Masters: Swing Lives
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra with Benny Carter and The Swing Masters: Swing Lives

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: I played on the piano with Bernard Addison... We played things like "The Toledo Shout."

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Another one that followed him in later years that I think was at the Grand Terrace was Lawrence Lucie, who we talked about, and Bernard Addison, who was an excellent swing man... But anyway, that was the rhythm section in Fletcher's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play with him quite a bit in Chicago... he had a guitarist by the name of Bernard Addison at this time... I would go there all the time because I just liked to play with Stuff and we would jam

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Let's see, according to Chilton he was with Rex Stewart's band, Earl McGee's orchestra, Teddy Hill, Albert Nicholas and Bernard Addison, and Louis Metcalf band.

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] With Ralph Escadero, Bernard Addison . . . >> [INTERVIEWEE] Tuba, Bernard Addison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: He had Bernard Addison playing guitar... Same time that Cozy Cole was with the band.
